title: Lock
abstract: A gravity actuated lock (10) includes: (a) a housing (12); (b) a latch retainer (14) that is movable between a first position and second position, and that is biased towards the first position; (c) a track (24); (d) an element (16) freely movable along the track (24) under the influence of gravity between: (i) a distal position, in which the element (16) is spaced from the latch retainer (14); and (ii) a proximal position, in which the element (16) is in contact with the latch retainer (14), (e) a latch member (18) that is movable between a locked position and an unlocked position, and that is biased towards the unlocked position, wherein the latch member (18): (i) may be captured by the latch retainer (14) when the latch member (18) is in the locked position and the latch retainer (14) is in the first position; and (ii) is released from the latch retainer (14) when the latch retainer (14) is in the second position, thereby permitting the latch member (18) to move from the locked position to the unlocked position; (f) a bolt (20); and (g) a receptacle (22) for at least partially receiving the bolt (20) therein. As the bolt (20) is received within the receptacle (22), the latch member (18) is caused to move from the unlocked position towards the locked position and become captured by the latch retainer (14), thereby securing the bolt within the receptacle (22). Whereas, as the lock (10) is inverted, the element (16) moves from the distal position to the proximal position, causing the latch retainer (14) to move towards the second position, and permitting the latch member (18) to move from the locked position towards the unlocked position, thereby permitting the bolt (20) to be released from the receptacle (22).
claims:
Claims CLAMS 1. A lock including: a housing; a latch retainer that is movable between a first position and second position, and that is biased towards the first position; a track; an element freely movable along the track under the influence of gravity between: (i) a distal position, in which the element is spaced from the latch retainer; and (is) a proximal position, in which the element is in contact with the latch retainer; a latch member that is movabie between a locked position and an unlocked position, and that is biased towards the unlocked position, wherein the latch member: (i) may be captured by the latch retainer when the latch member is in the locked position and the latch retainer is in the first position; and (is) is released from the latch retainer when the latch retainer is in the second position, thereby permitting the latch member to move from the locked position to the unlocked position; a bolt; and a receptacle for at feast partially receiving the bolt therein, such that: Ã¸) as the bolt is received within the receptacle, the latch member is caused to move from the unlocked position towards the locked position and become captured by the fetch retainer, thereby securing the bolt within the receptacle; and (ii) as the jock is inverted, the element moves from the distal position to the proximal position, causing the latch retainer to move towards the second position, and permitting the latch member to move from the locked position towards the unlocked position, thereby permitting the bolt to be released from the receptacle. 2. A lock according to claim 1, wherein the track includes an antechamber within which the element may be received whan the element is in the distal position, such that the Sock must fsrsi be tilted to move the element from the antechamber before the element is permitted to travel along the track under the influence of gravity S towards the proximal position, 3, A lock according to claim 2, wherein the latch retainer is resiliency movable from the first position towards the second position. 0 4. A lock according to claim 3, wherein the receptacle is defined by the housing, 5. A lock according to claim 4, wherein the iatch retainer includes a first formation that cooperates with a corresponding second formation on the latch member to capture the latch member when the latch retainer is in the first position. 5 6, A took according to ciatrn 5, wherein the bolt includes a third formation thai cooperates with a corresponding fourth formation on the iatch member to secure the bolt within the receptacle. 0 7, A lock according to claim 6, wherein the iatch member is pivotal!y connected to the housing so as to permit: (i) pivoting of the iatch member relative to the housing about the pivot axis; and (ii) radial displacement of the iatch member relative to the pivot axis by at least 2mm between a first radial position and a second radial position. S 8. A lock according to claim ?, wherein receipt of the bolt within the receptacle causes the iatch member to: (i) pivot about the pivot housing; and (is) be displaced from the first radial position towards the second radial position. 0 9. A lock according to claim 8 further including means for biasing the iatch member towards: (i) the unlocked position; and (ii) the first radial position, 10. A container including: 5 a bin; a shaft; 

 a fid hingsdty connected to the bin about the shaft; a harness including: a first connector at a first point an the harness, which first connector is secured to the shaft; a second connector at a second point on the harness, for facilitating securing of the second point of the harness to the bin; and a bolt at a third point on she harness, for facilitating securing of the third point of the harness to the bin; a link for securing either: (i) the second connector to the bin; or (Si) portions of the harness to each other; and a gravity actuated lock on the bin for receiving and reieasahly securing the bolt therein. 11. A container according to claim 10, wherein the bolt and gravity actuated lock comprises the lock according to any one of claims 1 to 9.
